---
title: Ruptures
---

Ruptures [@Truong2020] detects transitions by partitioning the time series into
homogeneous segments and finding breakpoints that minimize a cost function.
Ammonyte exposes six search algorithms (Pelt, Binseg, BottomUp, Dynp, Window,
and KernelCPD) and multiple cost functions, making it the most flexible of the
three methods.

## When to use

**Best for:** Sharp structural breaks in mean or variance, when flexibility in
algorithm and cost function choice is needed.

**Limitation:** Like the [Augmented KS Test](augmented_ks_test.qmd), ruptures
detects statistical breaks in amplitude. It says nothing about the dynamics,
for dynamically-sensitive detection, see [LERM](lerm.qmd).

## Reference

Truong, C., Oudre, L., & Vayatis, N. (2020). Selective review of offline
change point detection methods. *Signal Processing*, 167, 107299.
<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sigpro.2019.107299>

## Tutorial

See the [Ruptures Validation](../notebooks/Ruptures_Validation.ipynb) notebook
for a worked example.
